title: Retrieving Email Messages

Retrieving Email Messages

Retrieving Email Messages

You can retrieve MIME-compliant email messages by calling operations on the UPOPMAIL component.

  1. Instantiate the UPOPMAIL service.

    You can use the activate command or create a new instance with a handle.

    ;in a ProcScript module, for example, an operation, function, or Detail trigger
variables
 handle mailApiHandle
 string vHeaderList, vHeaderListPart, vHeader
 numeric vCounter, vItem
endvariables
newinstance "UPOPMAIL", mailApiHandle
  2. Logon to the POP mail server using the LogonMail operation. 
    MailerHandle2->LogonMail ("pop.mygreatcorp.net", "", "")
  3. Get a list of the email messages on the mail server using ListMail.

    The information in the list includes the header information and the length of the message, but not the message itself. ListMail only retrieves the first 10K of data, so if you want to retrieve the rest of the list, you need to use ListMailX repeatedly until all the data is retrieved.

    MailerHandle2->ListMail ($vHeaderList)
if ($status = -16) ; -16 indicates that data exceeds 10K
 repeat
 MailerHandle2->ListMailX (vHeaderListPart)
 if (vHeaderList)
 vHeaderList = "%%vHeaderList%%vHeaderListPart" ; Add the data to the existing email list 
 else
 vHeaderList = vHeaderListPart
 endif
 until ($status != ""-16")
endif
  4. Parse the header list so that you can get the message ID for any message you want to retrieve.

    In the following example, we create an occurrence of an entity called RECEIVED for each retrieved message header.

    ;initialize the numeric variables
vHeadersReceived = 1 
vItem = 1
;define a loop to create new occurrences of RECEIVED 
 while (vItem > 0)
 ;increment the counter for the number of messages received
 vHeadersReceived = vHeadersReceived+1 
 ;scan the header list for the total number of messages and set this as the item number
 vItem = $scan(vHeaderList,"NMB=%%vHeadersReceived%%%") 
 if (vItem > 0 )
 vHeader = vHeaderList[1:(vItem-2)] 
 ;set the starting location of the string
 vHeaderList = vHeaderList[vItem] 
 getlistitems/occ vHeader
 creocc "RECEIVED", vHeadersReceived
 else
 vHeader=vHeaderList
 getlistitems/occ vHeader
 endif
 endwhile
 setocc "RECEIVED" , 1
end
  5. Retrieve a specified message from the mail server. 
    MailerHandle2->GetMail (MESSAGEID.RECEIVED,ATTACHDIR.RECEIVED,"0",HEADER.RECEIVED, MAILTEXT.RECEIVED, ATTACHED.RECEIVED)
if ($status = -16) 
 MailerHandle2->GetMailX(MESSAGEID.RECEIVED,ATTACHDIR.RECEIVED,"0",HEADER.RECEIVED, vMessagePart, ATTACHED.RECEIVED))
 if (MAILTEXT.RECEIVED)
 MAILTEXT.RECEIVED = "%%MAILTEXT.RECEIVED%%vMessagePart"
 else
 MAILTEXT.RECEIVED = vMessagePart
 endif
endwhile
  6. Delete the mail from the server.
  7. Logoff the POP server. 
    MailerHandle2->LogoffMail
end; end operation
Related Links
  • activate

    Calls an operation on a component instance.

  • LogonMail

    Log on to the designated POP server using TCP or TLS.

  • ListMail and ListMailX

    Return a list of available messages on the POP3 server.

Logoff the POP server. MailerHandle2->LogoffMail end; end operation Copy Related Links activate Calls an operation on a component instance.
